mnemonics: S.V.N. - Scalability, Variety, Velocity. RDBMS fails on the three core V's of big data.
keyPoints: RDBMS is not designed for horizontal scalability required by massive big data volumes.
The rigid schema of RDBMS cannot handle the variety of unstructured and semi-structured data common in big data.
RDBMS is not optimized for the high velocity (speed) of data ingestion and real-time processing needed in big data analytics.
commonMistakes: Assuming RDBMS can be scaled easily to petabyte scale by just adding more hardware (vertical scaling has limits).
Trying to force unstructured big data (like social media posts) into a rigid, predefined relational schema, losing valuable information.
